violations.finding: Chinese characters in technical-prose gloss inside the M2 Score-injection echo contract section: 'The free-text action proves 行动 (act), not just 读 (read).' This is NOT a verbatim user-binding quote — it is a documentation gloss explaining English terms. Standard 6 unconditional scan applies. Introduced this cycle via M2 implementation (commit c75c34f).
violations.recommendation: Rewrite the sentence as: 'The free-text action proves action, not just reading.' Drop the parenthetical Chinese glosses entirely; the English already communicates the read/act dichotomy.

violations.finding: Identical Chinese-gloss violation as agents/dev.md:497 — '...proves 行动 (act), not just 读 (read).' in the M2 Score-injection echo contract section. Introduced this cycle via M2 (commit c75c34f).
violations.recommendation: Rewrite as: 'The free-text action proves action, not just reading.' Same fix as agents/dev.md:497; apply consistently across the three agent files that share this M2 paragraph (ba/dev/qa).

violations.finding: Identical Chinese-gloss violation as agents/dev.md:497 and agents/ba.md:777 — '...proves 行动 (act), not just 读 (read).' in the M2 Score-injection echo contract section. Introduced this cycle via M2 (commit c75c34f).
violations.recommendation: Rewrite as: 'The free-text action proves action, not just reading.' Same fix as the other two agent files.

violations.finding: Chinese verbatim user-quote embedded as parenthetical attribution inside English prose: 'The user has explicitly delegated content quality to the orchestrator's judgment ("自我总结的内容质量靠 orchestrator 即兴生成：无所谓").' The Standard 6 verbatim-user-quote exemption scopes such quotes to docs/dev/ticket-*.md only; this is in commands/close.md. The verbatim quote is already preserved at docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md (per codex finding — the quote is at line ~294 of that ticket; also at docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260524-205206.md). Introduced this cycle (commit c75c34f).
violations.recommendation: Replace the Chinese parenthetical with an English attribution that cites the artifact, e.g.: 'The user has explicitly delegated content quality to the orchestrator's judgment (original user wording preserved in docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md and docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260524-205206.md).' The verbatim quote remains accessible at the cited path; the command file stays in English per Standard 6.

violations.finding: M4.3 (this cycle) adds a NEW bare 'python3 -c' invocation inside the Layer A bulk-commit-auth-flag block: '_BULK_TARGET=$(echo "$INPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; ...")'. The Standard 3 catalog does not explicitly exempt hooks. Although other hook files use the same bare-python3 pattern (line 10 in this same file is pre-existing; pretool-bash-safety.sh uses a resolved ${PYTHON_BIN} fallback), the M4 block at line 19 is new code that perpetuates the inconsistency in scope-changed code.
violations.recommendation: Either (a) apply the pretool-bash-safety.sh PYTHON_BIN-resolution idiom at the top of pretool-write-guard.sh and replace bare 'python3' with '"$PYTHON_BIN"' on lines 10, 19, 38, 41, 47, 51, 66, 121; OR (b) document a formal hook-inline-stdlib-python exemption in Standard 3 of agents/style-inspector.md and apply it consistently. Recommended path: (a) — converges the two pretool hooks to the same idiom.
violations.rationale_blocks_current_diff_false: The pre-existing hook convention is bare 'python3' (line 10 of this file, lines 9-11 of stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh, all hooks in the repo). Hooks fire before venv may be available on a fresh checkout — they need standard-library Python only. The M4 line-19 addition follows the same in-file convention. Codex raised this in adversarial review; flagging for close-gate visibility without blocking the cycle, since (i) the pattern is not introduced by THIS diff (only one instance of it is), (ii) hooks are foundational infrastructure with venv-bootstrap concerns, and (iii) fixing requires touching pre-existing lines outside scope.

observations_only.finding: Chinese stderr line: 'BLOCKED: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！'. Standard 6 in-scope file; however this line is PRE-EXISTING (not introduced by THIS cycle's M4 diff which lives at Layer 1.F lines 633-671). Raised by codex as an observation; surfaced here without blocking.
observations_only.recommendation: Translate to English in a future cleanup cycle: 'BLOCKED: must use the normal UI flow to create a session; never permitted to create sessions via code/API.' Cycle-scope: not this task.

intentional_per_dispatch.description: EVENT_DELTAS table values — intentional Path A static rebalance per M1 (cycle-total cap +5 across {dev,ba,qa}). Not a Standard 2 hardcode violation: these are spec constants, not environment-specific paths.
bulk-commit-auth-flag-write deny block — intentional M4.3 security expansion per AC-04. Pattern-mention-based deny (not write-verb-based) is deliberate per codex Cycle-5 finding #2 hardening.

bulk-commit-auth-flag/sentinel Write+NotebookEdit deny block — intentional M4.3 security expansion per AC-04. ALL model tool calls denied regardless of agent_id is deliberate per codex iter-2 finding #7.
Bulk auth-flag reap + bulk-commit sentinel reap blocks — intentional M4.4 cleanup additions per AC-04.
COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE stderr marker contract — intentional observable trace per M5/AC-05 iter-2 remediation.

New reference harness embodying commit.md Step 7 SELECTION + TRACE algorithm. Not a Standard 7 (merge-similar-scripts) violation: the harness is the executable reference embodiment of the same algorithm documented in commit.md, satisfying AC-05 Phase B test contract — not a duplicate.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed all 4 draft Standard 6 findings as valid (no false positives) and severity correctly set to critical. Tightened recommendation for finding #4 (close.md:435) — quote is already preserved at docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md so the fix is replace-with-English-attribution, not relocate. Surfaced one missed Standard 3 candidate (pretool-write-guard.sh:19 bare python3 -c in new M4 code) and one OBSERVATION_ONLY (pre-existing Chinese stderr at pretool-bash-safety.sh:911).

findings.issue: Finding 4 fix needs tightening — verbatim quote is already preserved at docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md (~line 294); recommendation should be replace-with-English-attribution citing that path, not relocate the quote.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace Chinese parenthetical in commands/close.md:435 with: 'original user wording preserved in docs/dev/ticket-20260524-205206.md'.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct: the quote already exists in docs/dev artifacts. Updated finding #4 recommendation to cite the existing preservation path and use replace-with-English-attribution wording.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 3 issue: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:19 adds a new bare 'python3 -c' invocation as part of the M4.3 Layer A block. The catalog does not explicitly exempt hooks; the draft's 'pre-existing convention' rationale is informal.
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) flag as use-source-venv critical introduced_in_diff:true, or (b) formalize a hook-inline-stdlib-python exemption in Standard 3.
findings.rationale: Added as violation #5 with introduced_in_diff:true. Set blocks_current_diff:false with explicit rationale: (i) pre-existing hook-wide convention, (ii) hooks must fire before venv bootstrap on fresh checkouts, (iii) fixing requires touching lines outside the M4 scope. Surfaces the issue to close-gate visibility for adjudication without auto-blocking the cycle.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:911 contains Chinese stderr in a Standard 6 in-scope file but is pre-existing (not part of M4 diff). Should be surfaced if exhaustive reporting required.
findings.proposed_fix: Include as introduced_in_diff:false, blocks_current_diff:false in an observations_only section.
findings.rationale: Added to observations_only[] section with introduced_in_diff:false. Does not block this cycle; flagged for a future Standard 6 cleanup pass.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 codex findings incorporated. Finding #4 recommendation tightened. New violation #5 added for hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:19 (Standard 3) with introduced_in_diff:true blocks_current_diff:false to surface without hard-blocking. Pre-existing pretool-bash-safety.sh:911 Chinese stderr added to observations_only[].

verdict_plumbing_ac_2_6.rule_applied: AC-2.6 (b) line-level: 4 introduced_in_diff:true findings (english-only) provably overlap changed-line ranges in scope-listed files and are absent from pre-diff baseline (M2 paragraph and close.md M3 parenthetical were both introduced in commit c75c34f). 1 additional finding (use-source-venv at pretool-write-guard.sh:19) is introduced_in_diff:true but blocks_current_diff:false (advisory). Net blocking: 4 critical violations on changed lines.
advisory_only_for_close_gate: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:19 (use-source-venv — flagged with rationale, does not auto-block)
